title: Rent Payment Assistance * Families with Children - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rent Payment Assistance * Families with Children 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Disaster/Emergency Issues Domestic Violence Issues Domestic Violence Issues * African American Community Domestic Violence Issues * LGBTQ2+ Individuals Families with Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Veterans Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Mental Health Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Slavic Community Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants. Hours: No standard hours (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, rental deposit assistance, and rent application fee assistance along with housing case management services. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed and on a first come, first serve basis. Only the first 40 callers will receive callbacks. Rent assistance is limited to 2 months. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Familes with children under age 18 who are currently housed and an income level at or below 50% of Area Median Income (AMI) with an imminent risk of homelessness. Priority is given to SUN school families who identify as Asian Pacific Islanders or African Immigrants. Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 234-2048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE OHP HEALTH-RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers case management to help households navigate resources and search for housing. Eligible households are screened and supported through the OHP Health-Related Social Needs benefit. Additional rent and utility assistance may be available based on eligibility and funding availability. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4:30pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 687-2667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD OHP HEALTH-RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS
